history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible to the upper-right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The cell to my right is open floor, so I should turn right to face that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to the left side of my view. I am facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is open floor, while walls block movement to the right and lava is behind-left. Advancing forward is the safe move that brings me closer to the open corridor leading toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal lies to the left, but I am facing upward and the cell directly ahead is open floor. Moving forward would go away from the goal into unknown space, while the known corridor toward the goal is to my left. I should turn left to orient toward the goal path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left. The goal is straight ahead along the corridor, but there is one open floor cell before it. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, and moving forward advances toward the goal while avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left. The cell directly ahead contains lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The goal is still further left, but I must avoid the lava by changing orientation. Turning right will face me upward toward a potentially safe route around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my left, but the direct upper-left path is blocked by lava. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, and moving forward will position me to navigate around the lava while staying in the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
